guided.name: Open this folder in VS Code
guided.blurb: Copy a remote session's cwd to the Mac clipboard, then open it in local VS Code over SSH.

steps.detail: Tap the tmux thumb (prefix = Ctrl+Space) to arm a tmux command.
Prefix → o runs cpwd: copies $PWD to the Mac clipboard over SSH+tmux.
Cmd+Space opens Raycast on the host (universal launcher, works on the MacBook too).
Run the 'Open in VS Code' script command — blank arg pastes the cwd and opens vscode://ssh-remote+raf-dev<path>.

guided.name: New tmux window
guided.blurb: Spin up a fresh window in the current tmux session.

steps.detail: Tap the tmux thumb to send the prefix (Ctrl+Space).
Prefix → c creates a new window; switch with prefix n / p / 1-9.

guided.name: Split & navigate tmux panes
guided.blurb: Split the current window, then move between panes vim-style.

steps.detail: Tap the tmux thumb to send the prefix.
Prefix → v (custom bind for split-right). Prefix → b splits down.
Prefix → b splits the pane downward.
Prefix → h/j/k/l jumps left/down/up/right between panes (vim directions).

guided.name: Copy the current directory
guided.blurb: Put a remote session's working directory on the Mac clipboard via OSC52.

steps.detail: Tap the tmux thumb to arm a tmux command.
Prefix → o runs cpwd — copies $PWD to the Mac clipboard over SSH+tmux (OSC52). Paste it anywhere.

guided.name: Jump to an app
guided.blurb: Hold the Hyper thumb, pick an app with your right hand — go-to-app in one chord (Glove80 speed-up; Cmd+Space + type on the MacBook).

steps.detail: Hold the left Hyper thumb. Right-hand letters become go-to-app chords.
Hyper+J focuses (or opens) the terminal.
Hyper+K jumps to the editor.
Hyper+L jumps to Linear.
The rest of the right-hand grid — pure adjacency, no typing.

guided.name: Rebuild the dev container
guided.blurb: Rebuild the mega-container image and confirm it's healthy — without wiping Tailscale state.

steps.detail: Connect over Tailscale (hostname may be raf-dev-1, etc.), then ws to attach tmux.
~/mega-container/rebuild.sh rebuilds with --pull and force-recreates (volumes preserved). Never docker compose down -v.
rebuild.sh runs mega-doctor at the end — verifies binaries, secrets, mounts, MCPs, OpenCode web.

guided.name: Start a task → draft PR
guided.blurb: Spin up an isolated worktree from a Linear issue, do the work, and open a draft self-assigned PR.

steps.detail: Sets up (or re-enters) a worktree for the issue and cds in. Add --go to run hands-off through to a draft PR.
Make the change, run the verification live (KTD14) before moving on.
Creates a draft, self-assigned PR with the Linear tag auto-detected in the title.

guided.name: Morning triage
guided.blurb: Start the day: Gmail notifications enriched with GitHub / Sentry / Linear / Slack context.

steps.detail: Pulls Gmail notifications and enriches each with GitHub, Sentry, Linear, and Slack context.
Move through the triaged items; act on what matters, archive the rest.
Generate the standup from git/Linear/Toggl and post to #ra-lab-notes-rafael on confirm.

services.name: Warp
services.note: tmux prefix is Ctrl+Space. Bold tmux rows live on the ZMK tmux layer.

services.name: tmux
services.note: Custom config; prefix = Ctrl+Space. Bold rows are on the ZMK tmux layer.

services.name: Gmail
services.note: Settings → Keyboard shortcuts ON. Vimium excluded-keys for mail.google.com/* = ?jknpxercgilsafd#ubm/

services.name: GitHub
services.note: Reach the window with Cmd+Shift+A (tab search) and Cmd+` (cycle windows).

services.name: VS Code
services.note: Git review + plan reading. Custom binds in keybindings.json: stageFile→Cmd+K Cmd+A, push→Cmd+K Cmd+P, GitLens changed-file Opt+]/Opt+[.

bindings.keys: Cmd+K Cmd+Opt+S / Cmd+K Cmd+U / Cmd+K Cmd+R

workflows.name: Shell functions
workflows.note: macOS-only helpers (brew-up, warp-*, metr-*) are gated behind darwin; not in the Linux container.

items.name: awstg [env]
items.does: Set AWS_PROFILE=staging and ENVIRONMENT (default 'staging', e.g. awstg dev4)

items.name: awspr
items.does: Set AWS_PROFILE and ENVIRONMENT to production

items.name: my-chezmoi
items.does: cd into the chezmoi source dir (~/.local/share/chezmoi)

items.name: loadenv <file>
items.does: Export all env vars from a file (e.g. loadenv .env)

items.name: minikube_reset
items.does: Reset minikube for METR/inspect-action (compose down, delete cluster, re-run start-minikube.sh)

items.name: ws [name]
items.does: Create or attach a durable tmux session on the 'work' socket (default 'main'), nesting-safe

items.name: wsl
items.does: List tmux sessions on the 'work' socket

items.name: brew-up
items.does: macOS: brew update + upgrade + cleanup

items.name: warp-tab [path]
items.does: macOS: open a new Warp tab at the given/current directory

items.name: warp-window [path]
items.does: macOS: open a new Warp window at the given/current directory

items.name: metr-dev
items.does: macOS: open the 4-tab METR dev workspace (inspect-action, mp4-deploy, metr-iam, chezmoi)

items.name: metr-deploy
items.does: macOS: open the 2-tab METR deploy workspace (inspect-action, mp4-deploy)

items.name: cpwd
items.does: Copy $PWD to the Mac clipboard via OSC52 over SSH+tmux (for Raycast 'Open in VS Code')

items.name: oc [args]
items.does: Run OpenCode with auto-assigned port and registry tracking for the session-registry plugin

items.name: occ
items.does: OpenCode --continue (resume the last session)

items.name: oc ps [--json]
items.does: List running OpenCode instances (PID, port, dir, session, title); prunes dead entries

items.name: oc history [HOURS] [--json]
items.does: List recent OpenCode sessions from the local DB (default last 24h)

workflows.name: Container ops
workflows.note: Mega-container dev environment. NEVER docker compose down -v — it wipes Tailscale state and forces a new hostname.

items.name: ~/mega-container/start.sh
items.does: Routine start: fetch 1Password token, compose up, wait for bootstrap, check Tailscale (no rebuild)

items.name: ~/mega-container/rebuild.sh
items.does: Rebuild image with --pull, force-recreate (preserves volumes), run full mega-doctor

items.name: mega-doctor [--quick]
items.does: One-shot health check (binaries, secrets, mounts, MCPs, opencode web); --quick skips network probes

items.name: /hot-patch [--chezmoi]
items.does: Update a running container without rebuild (chezmoi + mise + plugins); --chezmoi for just dotfiles

items.name: ssh raf-dev
items.does: SSH into the container via Tailscale (hostname may be raf-dev-1, etc.); then ws to attach tmux

items.name: OpenCode web
items.does: Browser UI at https://raf-dev.koi-moth.ts.net (tailnet-only, works from phone, skips tmux)

items.name: docker compose exec mega tailscale up --ssh ...
items.does: One-time Tailscale SSH auth (and to re-enable SSH if connection is refused)

workflows.name: Claude skills
workflows.note: Slash-commands from chezmoi commands/. Plugin skills (compound-engineering:*, superpowers:*) come from plugins, not listed here.

items.name: /async-standup
items.does: Generate an async standup from git, Linear, and Toggl; review then post to Slack (#ra-lab-notes-rafael), falling back to a file

items.name: /aws-preflight
items.does: Pre-flight checks before AWS operations

items.name: /aws-switch
items.does: Switch AWS profile and verify credentials

items.name: /claude-diagnostics
items.does: Run diagnostics and generate a debug report for Claude Code issues

items.name: /claude-update
items.does: Update Claude Code and sync dotfiles from chezmoi

items.name: /claude-validate
items.does: Validate Claude Code setup and configuration

items.name: /dev-clean
items.does: Clean up merged/stale git worktrees across all repos

items.name: /dev-list
items.does: List all git worktrees across all repos

items.name: /ecr-login
items.does: Authenticate Docker to AWS ECR for image push/pull

items.name: /fill-toggl
items.does: Auto-fill missing Toggl time entries: aggregate data, then classify and create entries (supports --dry-run, --auto)

items.name: /hot-patch
items.does: Hot-patch the container without a rebuild (dotfiles, tools, packages)

items.name: /k8s-reset
items.does: Reset the Kubernetes cluster to a clean state

items.name: /k8s-switch
items.does: Switch Kubernetes context by inferring the target cluster from AWS account/repo/contexts, then update kubeconfig

items.name: /linear-triage
items.does: Triage Linear issues - inbox, backlog, update, create

items.name: /load-env
items.does: Load environment variables from a .env file

items.name: /mcp-setup-1password
items.does: Interactive setup for MCP credentials in 1Password

items.name: /morning-triage
items.does: Daily morning triage - Gmail notifications enriched with GitHub/Sentry/Linear/Slack

items.name: /plan-review
items.does: Review a local plan/doc with the human via the self-hosted Proof editor (localhost only)

items.name: /pr-create
items.does: Create a PR with an auto-detected Linear issue tag in the title (draft + self-assigned)

items.name: /sentry-triage
items.does: Triage Sentry issues - investigate, bulk actions, create Linear issues

items.name: /setup-work
items.does: Set up or re-enter an isolated worktree for a task and cd in, then STOP (--go works through to a draft self-assigned PR)
